_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Closed Reduction and Percutaneous Screw Fixation
min_doctor_qualification: Diploma in Orthopedics with 5 years' experience
clinical_indications: Garden Type I (incomplete fracture - valgus impacted-nondisplaced) of femoral neck
Garden Type II (complete fracture - nondisplaced) of femoral neck

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
X-ray/CT (Hemiarthroplasty) labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) affected part

claim.label: Post procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) affected
Invoice and bar code of Implant
Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge

ppd_preauth.q: Was the clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission submitted?
Clinical photograph of affected part submitted?
X-ray/CT (Hemiarthroplasty), labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) of affected part submitted?
Was the clinical notes and imaging report are indicative of surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Are detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) submitted?
Post procedure X-ray labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) affected part submitted?
Is the Invoice/barcode of Implant used submitted?
Is there a Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes and/or imaging report not indicative of surgery.
X-ray/CT is missing or not labelled with patient ID, date, or affected side (Left/Right) during pre-authorization.
Post-procedure X-ray is missing or not labelled with patient ID, date, or affected side (Left/Right) during claim submission.
Invoice and barcode of implant are missing at the time of claim submission.
Clinical notes are incomplete or missing key information such as indication for procedure or planned line of management.
Clinical photograph of affected part is missing or unclear.

clinical_key_pointers: Common injuries, especially seen in elderly in the emergency settings, also in young patients in sports or high energy trauma.
Femoral neck fractures associated with multiple injuries and high rates of avascular necrosis and nonunion.
There are many femoral neck fractures and the most common classification is by Garden:
Type I: Incomplete fracture - valgus impacted-nondisplaced
Type III: Complete fracture - partial displaced
Type IV: Complete fracture - fully displaced
Pauwel: Type I less than 30 degrees, Type II 30-50 degrees, Type III greater than 50 degrees
surgical_options: Non-displaced fractures are treated with typically with percutaneous cannulated screws or a sliding hip screw.
With displaced fractures of the femoral neck: Less active individuals may receive a hemiarthroplasty.
With displaced fractures of the femoral neck: More active individuals are treated with total hip arthroplasty.
